title: Safely Store `MsExperiment` Objects in a Portable Stash
text: Data objects R can serialized disk R’s rds RData format using base R save() function re-imported using load() function. R-specific binary data format can however used easily programming languages preventing exchange R data objects software. MsStash package defines basic classes generic methods export import mass spectrometry (MS) data objects various storage formats aiming facilitate data exchange software. MsExperimentStash package implements portable data storage formats (stashes) data classes MsExperiment package, including MsExperiment object. Supported stash formats , next storage simple plain text files, also Bioconductor’s alabaster format defined alabaster.base related packages.

title: Safely Store `MsExperiment` Objects in a Portable Stash
text: package can installed BiocManager package. install BiocManager use install.packages("BiocManager") , , BiocManager::install("RforMassSpectrometry/MsExperimentStash") install package.

title: Safely Store `MsExperiment` Objects in a Portable Stash
text: MS data objects can saved restored saveMsObject() readMsObject() functions () MS data stashes. Supported stash formats respective parameter objects : AlabasterParam: storage MS data using Bioconductor’s alabaster.base framework using files HDF5 JSON format. MS stashes format fully support functions saveObject() readObject() alabaster.base. PlainTextParam: storage data (custom) plain text file format. Note format currently support data structures potentially present MsExperiment hence alabaster format preferred. See also vignette MsStash details formats implementation notes. example create MsExperiment object MS data two example MS data files MsDataHub package. next create SummarizedExperiment add MsExperiment object. real-world use case contain quantitative feature abundances e.g. preprocessing data xcms. example fill SummarizedExperiment arbitrary information random abundance values. next store MsExperiment object MsExperimentStash using saveMsObject() function. use alabaster format define location stash path parameter AlabasterParam. present example save temporary folder. content stash folder : alabaster format, slot MsExperiment object stored sub directory. Spectra object representing experiment’s MS data stored example (SpectraStash) sub-folder spectra. general, users interact directly files stash, restore stashed MsExperiment MsExperimentStash using readMsObject() function: readMsObject() need specify type object restore stash first parameter function - case MsExperiment(). MsExperimentStash adds full support alabaster-based serialization formats MsExperiment objects can therefore also use readObject() alabaster.base package restore object. Due modular structure MsExperimentStash can load also single component MsExperiment. can example restore Spectra object spectra sub-folder: SummarizedExperiment qdata sub-folder (using alabaster.base functions):
code: library(MsExperiment) library(MsExperimentStash) library(MsDataHub) fls <- c(X20171016_POOL_POS_1_105.134.mzML(),          X20171016_POOL_POS_3_105.134.mzML())  #' Define a data.frame providing information on samples d <- data.frame(name = c("QC 1", "QC 2"),                 sample_type = c("QC POOL", "QC POOL"),                 injection_index = c(1, 8))  #' Read the data as an MsExperiment object mse <- readMsExperiment(fls, sampleData = d) mse ## Object of class MsExperiment  ##  Spectra: MS1 (1862)  ##  Experiment data: 2 sample(s) ##  Sample data links: ##   - spectra: 2 sample(s) to 1862 element(s). #' Define a SummarizedExperiment with quantification data library(SummarizedExperiment) se <- SummarizedExperiment(     list(raw = matrix(rnorm(8), ncol = 2)),     rowData = data.frame(feature_id = c("F01", "F02", "F03", "F04"),                          mzmed = c(127.2, 232.1, 321.2, 134.5),                          rtmed = c(38.5, 127.3, 219.8, 64.3)),     colData = d) rownames(se) <- c("F01", "F02", "F03", "F04") colnames(se) <- c("QC_1", "QC_2")  #' Add the SummarizedExperiment to the MsExperiment qdata(mse) <- se mse ## Object of class MsExperiment  ##  Spectra: MS1 (1862)  ##  SummarizedExperiment: 4 feature(s) ##  Experiment data: 2 sample(s) ##  Sample data links: ##   - spectra: 2 sample(s) to 1862 element(s). #' Define the location of the stash d <- file.path(tempfile(), "mse_stash")  #' Configure the format and location ap <- AlabasterParam(d)  #' Save the `MsExperiment` object to the stash saveMsObject(mse, ap) library(fs) dir_tree(d) ## /tmp/RtmpirqO2C/file12bf665e0799/mse_stash ## ├── OBJECT ## ├── _environment.json ## ├── experiment_files ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── x ## │       ├── OBJECT ## │       └── list_contents.json.gz ## ├── metadata ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── list_contents.json.gz ## ├── other_data ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── list_contents.json.gz ## ├── qdata ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   ├── assays ## │   │   ├── 0 ## │   │   │   ├── OBJECT ## │   │   │   └── array.h5 ## │   │   └── names.json ## │   ├── column_data ## │   │   ├── OBJECT ## │   │   └── basic_columns.h5 ## │   └── row_data ## │       ├── OBJECT ## │       └── basic_columns.h5 ## ├── sample_data ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── basic_columns.h5 ## ├── sample_data_links ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   ├── list_contents.json.gz ## │   └── other_contents ## │       └── 0 ## │           ├── OBJECT ## │           └── array.h5 ## ├── sample_data_links_mcols ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── basic_columns.h5 ## └── spectra ##     ├── OBJECT ##     ├── backend ##     │   ├── OBJECT ##     │   └── spectra_data ##     │       ├── OBJECT ##     │       └── basic_columns.h5 ##     ├── metadata ##     │   ├── OBJECT ##     │   └── list_contents.json.gz ##     ├── processing ##     │   ├── OBJECT ##     │   └── contents.h5 ##     ├── processing_chunk_size ##     │   ├── OBJECT ##     │   └── contents.h5 ##     ├── processing_queue_variables ##     │   ├── OBJECT ##     │   └── contents.h5 ##     └── spectra_processing_queue.json res <- readMsObject(MsExperiment(), ap) res ## Object of class MsExperiment  ##  Spectra: MS1 (1862)  ##  SummarizedExperiment: 4 feature(s) ##  Experiment data: 2 sample(s) ##  Sample data links: ##   - spectra: 2 sample(s) to 1862 element(s). library(alabaster.base) res <- readObject(d) res ## Object of class MsExperiment  ##  Spectra: MS1 (1862)  ##  SummarizedExperiment: 4 feature(s) ##  Experiment data: 2 sample(s) ##  Sample data links: ##   - spectra: 2 sample(s) to 1862 element(s). library(Spectra) sps <- readMsObject(Spectra(), AlabasterParam(file.path(d, "spectra"))) sps ## MSn data (Spectra) with 1862 spectra in a MsBackendMzR backend: ##        msLevel     rtime scanIndex ##      <integer> <numeric> <integer> ## 1            1     0.280         1 ## 2            1     0.559         2 ## 3            1     0.838         3 ## 4            1     1.117         4 ## 5            1     1.396         5 ## ...        ...       ...       ... ## 1858         1   258.636       927 ## 1859         1   258.915       928 ## 1860         1   259.194       929 ## 1861         1   259.473       930 ## 1862         1   259.752       931 ##  ... 25 more variables/columns. ##  ## file(s): ## 108862219686_7859 ## 10883dc0fa5b_7860 readObject(file.path(d, "qdata")) ## class: SummarizedExperiment  ## dim: 4 2  ## metadata(0): ## assays(1): raw ## rownames(4): F01 F02 F03 F04 ## rowData names(3): feature_id mzmed rtmed ## colnames(2): QC_1 QC_2 ## colData names(3): name sample_type injection_index

title: Safely Store `MsExperiment` Objects in a Portable Stash
text: MS data example MsExperiment represented Spectra object using MsBackendMzR backend. type backend keeps spectra metadata memory mass peaks data (m/z intensity values) retrieved demand original MS data files. default, saved stash, metadata reference original MS data tiles serialized disk. MS data files moved another folder, MsExperimentStash moved another computer, data can fully restored (unless path new location MS data files provided parameter spectraPath readMsObject()). stash functionality Spectra backend implementation supports however parameter consolidate , set TRUE copy required data stash generating hence self-contained portable MsExperimentStash: SpectraStash within MsExperimentStash contains now also original MS data files (case random names without expected mzML file ending, data provided MsDataHub package): self-contained, size stash might become large, depending number size original MS data files. Alternatively, also change backend Spectra within MsExperiment -memory backend create stash object. full MS data now stored peaks.h5 file (HDF5 file format) within stash.
code: spectra(mse) ## MSn data (Spectra) with 1862 spectra in a MsBackendMzR backend: ##        msLevel     rtime scanIndex ##      <integer> <numeric> <integer> ## 1            1     0.280         1 ## 2            1     0.559         2 ## 3            1     0.838         3 ## 4            1     1.117         4 ## 5            1     1.396         5 ## ...        ...       ...       ... ## 1858         1   258.636       927 ## 1859         1   258.915       928 ## 1860         1   259.194       929 ## 1861         1   259.473       930 ## 1862         1   259.752       931 ##  ... 34 more variables/columns. ##  ## file(s): ## 108862219686_7859 ## 10883dc0fa5b_7860 #' Save the `MsExperiment` to a stash which includes the full data d <- file.path(tempdir(), "portable_stash")  saveMsObject(mse, AlabasterParam(d), consolidate = TRUE) dir_tree(file.path(d, "spectra", "backend")) ## /tmp/RtmpirqO2C/portable_stash/spectra/backend ## ├── 10883dc0fa5b_7860 ## ├── 108862219686_7859 ## ├── OBJECT ## └── spectra_data ##     ├── OBJECT ##     └── basic_columns.h5 #' Change the Spectra backend to MsBackendMemory: load all MS data #' into memory spectra(mse) <- setBackend(spectra(mse), MsBackendMemory())  #' Save the MsExperiment to a stash d <- file.path(tempdir(), "memory_stash") saveMsObject(mse, AlabasterParam(d)) dir_tree(file.path(d, "spectra", "backend")) ## /tmp/RtmpirqO2C/memory_stash/spectra/backend ## ├── OBJECT ## └── backend ##     ├── OBJECT ##     ├── mod_count ##     │   ├── OBJECT ##     │   └── contents.h5 ##     ├── peaks.h5 ##     └── spectra_data ##         ├── OBJECT ##         └── basic_columns.h5

title: Safely Store `MsExperiment` Objects in a Portable Stash
text: MetaboLights database one main repositories deposit metabolomics data sets experiments. readMsObject() MetaboLightsParam possible retrieve load data set MetaboLights study directly MsExperiment object. Sample protocol/metadata information loaded object’s sampleData() MS data files downloaded locally cached MsBackendMetaboLights package. data available object’s spectra() data. , demonstrate load dataset ID: MTBLS575. also use assayName parameter specify assay want load, filePattern parameter indicate assay files load. Defining assay name required studies one assay (e.g., data measured positive polarity modes using different chromatographic setups). filePattern hand allows restrict downloading specific files; example load data files file ending cdf. recommended adjust settings according specific study. Next, examine sampleData() mse object: observe large number columns present. Several parameters available readMsObject() function simplify restrict content loaded sampleData. Setting keepOntology = FALSE example remove columns related ontology terms, keepProtocol = FALSE remove columns related protocol information. simplify = TRUE option (default) removes NAs merges columns different names duplicate contents. can set simplify = FALSE retain columns. , load object , time simplifying sampleData: Note MS data files loaded local cache downloaded . Now, examine sampleData information: can see much simpler.
code: library(MsExperiment) #' Prepare parameter param <- MetaboLightsParam(     mtblsId = "MTBLS575",     assayName = paste0("a_MTBLS575_POS_INFEST_CTRL_mass_spectrometry.txt"),     filePattern = "cdf$")  #' Load MsExperiment object mse <- readMsObject(MsExperiment(), param) sampleData(mse) ## DataFrame with 6 rows and 30 columns ##     Sample Name Protocol REF Protocol REF.1 ##     <character>  <character>    <character> ## 1     PB130_co1   Extraction  Chromatogr... ## 2     PB130_co2   Extraction  Chromatogr... ## 3     PB130_co3   Extraction  Chromatogr... ## 4 PB130_sesa...   Extraction  Chromatogr... ## 5 PB130_sesa...   Extraction  Chromatogr... ## 6 PB130_sesa...   Extraction  Chromatogr... ##   Parameter Value[Chromatography Instrument] Parameter Value[Column model] ##                                  <character>                   <character> ## 1                              Waters ACQ...                 ACQUITY UP... ## 2                              Waters ACQ...                 ACQUITY UP... ## 3                              Waters ACQ...                 ACQUITY UP... ## 4                              Waters ACQ...                 ACQUITY UP... ## 5                              Waters ACQ...                 ACQUITY UP... ## 6                              Waters ACQ...                 ACQUITY UP... ##   Parameter Value[Column type] Protocol REF.2 Parameter Value[Scan polarity] ##                    <character>    <character>                    <character> ## 1                reverse ph...  Mass spect...                       positive ## 2                reverse ph...  Mass spect...                       positive ## 3                reverse ph...  Mass spect...                       positive ## 4                reverse ph...  Mass spect...                       positive ## 5                reverse ph...  Mass spect...                       positive ## 6                reverse ph...  Mass spect...                       positive ##   Parameter Value[Instrument] Parameter Value[Ion source] Term Source REF ##                   <character>                 <character>     <character> ## 1               Waters SYN...               electrospr... MS ## 6               Waters SYN...               electrospr...              MS ##   Term Accession Number Parameter Value[Mass analyzer] Raw_Spectral_Data_File ##             <character>                    <character>            <character> ## 1         http://pur...                  quadrupole...          FILES/PB13... ## 2         http://pur...                  quadrupole...          FILES/PB13... ## 3         http://pur...                  quadrupole... Metabolite...              m_MTBLS575...    MBG-CSIC ## 2  Data trans...  Metabolite...              m_MTBLS575...    MBG-CSIC ## 3  Data trans...  Metabolite...              m_MTBLS575... EFO           http://pur... ## 2            Zea mays s...               EFO           http://pur... ## 3            Zea mays s... Sample col... ## 2                  stem inter...           http://pur...  Sample col... ## 3                  stem inter...           http://pur... FILES/PB13...              m_MTBLS575... title: Serialize and restore MsExperiment objects in interoperable file formats
text: MsStash defines framework flexible, language-agnostic import export formats mass spectrometry (MS) data objects R. MsExperimentStash package extends framework implements functionality stash (restore) MsExperiment objects.

title: Serialize and restore MsExperiment objects in interoperable file formats
text: 📦 Store/restore MsExperiment objects /portable, language agnostic storage formats (stash) 🧩 Modular design via S4 parameter classes generic methods 🔄 Fully integrated Bioconductor’s alabaster.base package.

what: Load Content from a MetaboLights Study — MetaboLightsParam
title: Load Content from a MetaboLights Study — MetaboLightsParam
text: MetaboLightsParam class associated readMsObject() method allow users load MsExperiment::MsExperiment object study MetaboLights database (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metabolights/index) providing unique study identifier (parameter mtblsId). function particularly useful directly importing metabolomics data MsExperiment object analysis R environment. important note present possible read (import) data MetaboLights, save data MetaboLights. study contains multiple assays (e.g. measurements performed positive negative polarity data subsets different liquid chromatography setups used), user prompted select assay load. resulting MsExperiment object include sampleData slot populated data extracted selected assay. Users can define filter sampleData table specifying parameters. keepOntology parameter set TRUE default, meaning ontology-related columns retained. set FALSE, removed. ontology columns kept, column names may duplicated therefore numbered. order columns important, reflects assay sample information available MetaboLights. keepProtocol parameter also set TRUE default, meaning columns related protocols kept. set FALSE, removed. simplify parameter (default simplify = TRUE) allows define whether duplicated columns columns containing missing values removed. case duplicated content, first occurring column retained. filtering can performed using filePattern parameter MetaboLightsParam object. default parameter "mzML$|CDF$|cdf$|mzXML$", corresponds supported raw data file types.

title: Load Content from a MetaboLights Study — MetaboLightsParam
code: MetaboLightsParam(   mtblsId = character(),   assayName = character(),   filePattern = "mzML$|CDF$|cdf$|mzXML$" )  # S4 method for class 'MsExperiment,MetaboLightsParam' readMsObject(   object,   param,   keepOntology = TRUE,   keepProtocol = TRUE,   simplify = TRUE,   ... )

title: Load Content from a MetaboLights Study — MetaboLightsParam
text: mtblsId character(1) MetaboLights study ID, start "MTBL". identifier uniquely specifies study within MetaboLights database. assayName character(1) name assay load. study contains multiple assays parameter specified, user prompted select assay load. filePattern character(1) regular expression pattern filter raw data files associated selected assay. default value "mzML$|CDF$|cdf$|mzXML$", corresponding supported raw data file types. object readMsObject(): MsExperiment instance. param readMsObject(): MetaboLightsParam object. keepOntology logical(1) Whether keep columns related ontology object's sampleData(). Default TRUE. keepProtocol logical(1) Whether keep columns related protocols information object's sampleData(). Default TRUE. simplify logical(1) Whether simplify sampleData() table removing columns filled NAs duplicated content. Default TRUE. ... Currently ignored.

title: Load Content from a MetaboLights Study — MetaboLightsParam
text: readMsObject() returns MsExperiment object sampleData() populated MetaboLights sample assay information experiment's MS data loaded Spectra::Spectra object.

title: Load Content from a MetaboLights Study — MetaboLightsParam
text: Philippine Louail

title: Load Content from a MetaboLights Study — MetaboLightsParam
code: library(MsExperiment) #> Loading required package: ProtGenerics #>  #> Attaching package: ‘ProtGenerics’ #> The following object is masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     smooth ## Load a study with the mtblsId "MTBLS39" and selecting specific file ## pattern as well as removing ontology and protocol information in the ## metadata. param <- MetaboLightsParam(mtblsId = "MTBLS39", filePattern = "63A.cdf") ms_experiment <- readMsObject(MsExperiment(), param, keepOntology = FALSE,                               keepProtocol = FALSE) #> Only one assay file found: a_MTBLS39_the_plasticity_of_the_grapevine_berry_transcriptome_metabolite_profiling_mass_spectrometry.txt #> Used data files from the assay's column "Raw Spectral Data File" since none were available in column "Derived Spectral Data File". ms_experiment #> Object of class MsExperiment  #>  Spectra: MS1 (1664)  #>  Experiment data: 3 sample(s) #>  Sample data links: #>   - spectra: 3 sample(s) to 1664 element(s).  ## The object's sampleData contains information loaded from MetaboLights sampleData(ms_experiment) #> DataFrame with 3 rows and 9 columns #>   Sample Name Raw_Spectral_Data_File Metabolite Assignment File   Source Name #>   <character>            <character>                <character>   <character> #> 1      MN063A          FILES/MN06...              m_MTBLS39_... Vineyard M... #> 2      CS063A          FILES/CS06...              m_MTBLS39_... Vineyard C... #> 3      AM063A          FILES/AM06...              m_MTBLS39_... Vineyard A... #>   Characteristics[Organism] Characteristics[Organism part] #>                 <character>                    <character> #> 1             Vitis vini...                          berry #> 2             Vitis vini...                          berry #> 3             Vitis vini...                          berry #>   Factor Value[Year of Harvesting] Factor Value[Vineyard] #>                          <integer>            <character> #> 1                             2006          Vineyard M... #> 2                             2006          Vineyard C... #> 3                             2006          Vineyard A... #>   Factor Value[Replicate] #>               <character> #> 1                       A #> 2                       A #> 3                       A  ## The MS data files were downloaded and cached; the data is available ## through the object's `Spectra` spectra(ms_experiment) #> MSn data (Spectra) with 1664 spectra in a MsBackendMetaboLights backend: #>        msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>      <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1            1  0.296384         1 #> 2            1  6.206912         2 #> 3            1 12.093056         3 #> 4            1 17.942912         4 #> 5            1 23.835072         5 #> ...        ...       ...       ... #> 1660         1   2678.27       549 #> 1661         1   2683.01       550 #> 1662         1   2687.81       551 #> 1663         1   2692.62       552 #> 1664         1   2697.40       553 #>  ... 37 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> MN063A.cdf #> CS063A.cdf #> AM063A.cdf

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
text: MsExperiment::MsExperimentFiles class stores files (rather file names) part mass spectrometry experiment. supported stash formats MsExperimentFiles objects listed sections .

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
code: # S4 method for class 'MsExperimentFiles,PlainTextParam' saveMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsExperimentFiles,PlainTextParam' readMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsExperimentFiles' saveObject(x, path, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsExperimentFiles,AlabasterParam' saveMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsExperimentFiles,AlabasterParam' readMsObject(object, param, ...)

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
text: object MsExperimentFiles object. param MsStash::AlabasterParam MsStash::PlainTextParam. ... Currently ignored. x MsExperimentFiles object. path saveObject(): character(1) path object stored .

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
text: readMsObject() returns MsExperiment::MsExperimentFiles object.

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
text: MsExperimentFiles stash folder contains alabaster-specific OBJECT file sub-folder x MsExperimentFiles content serialized alabaster.base.

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
text: text-file format stash folder MsExperimentFile objects contains file ms_experiment_files.txt two tabulator separated columns name files. row (except first) one element MsExperimentFile, first defining object's names second content, represents characted vector file name(s), separated "|" (one).

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
text: Johannes Rainer

title: Stash for MsExperimentFiles — MsExperimentFilesStash
code: library(MsExperiment)  fls <- MsExperimentFiles(list(input = c("file.mzML", "file2.mgf")))  ## Define the path to the stash d <- file.path(tempdir(), "ms_file_stash")  ## Stash the object in alabaster format saveMsObject(fls, AlabasterParam(d))  ## The content of the stash: subfolder x contains the *character list* ## saved through the *alabaster.base* package. library(fs) dir_tree(d) #> /tmp/RtmpB00ZqG/ms_file_stash #> ├── OBJECT #> ├── _environment.json #> └── x #>     ├── OBJECT #>     └── list_contents.json.gz  ## Restore the object from stash res <- readMsObject(MsExperimentFiles(), AlabasterParam(d)) res #> MsExperimentFiles of length  1  #> [["input"]] file.mzML file2.mgf  ## In addition, it is possible to read the object also with the ## *alabaster.base* functionality library(alabaster.base) res <- readObject(d)

title: MsExperiment Stash — MsExperimentStash
text: MsExperiment objects can stored (read ) MsExperimentStashes using saveMsObject() readMsObject() functions take second argument parameter select configure format stash. supported stash formats listed sections .

title: MsExperiment Stash — MsExperimentStash
code: # S4 method for class 'MsExperiment,PlainTextParam' saveMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsExperiment,PlainTextParam' readMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsExperiment' saveObject(x, path, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsExperiment,AlabasterParam' saveMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsExperiment,AlabasterParam' readMsObject(object, param, ...)

title: MsExperiment Stash — MsExperimentStash
text: object MsExperiment object. param parameter object select configure stash format. Either MsStash::AlabasterParam MsStash::PlainTextParam. ... saveMsObject(): optional arguments passed saveMsObject() function stash Spectra object (present), consolidate. readMsObject(): optional arguments readMsObject() call restore Spectra object (spectraPath). See SpectraStash::SpectraStash information. x MsExperiment object. path saveObject():

title: MsExperiment Stash — MsExperimentStash
text: Overwriting existing MsExperimentStash allowed. Serializing MsExperiment objects containing QFeatures object currently supported. plain text file-based stash currently supported.

title: MsExperiment Stash — MsExperimentStash
text: stash format complete reliable way long-term (portable) storage MsExperiment. Objects can saved read stash format either using saveMsObject() readMsObject() functions also using alabaster.base::saveObject() alabaster.base::readObject() functions. Data object's slots stored respective folders (using alabaster functionality). folders : experiment_files: content @experimentFiles slot, stored MsExperimentFilesStash. metadata: content object's @metadata slot. other_data: content object's @otherData slot. Note export fails object types stored slot without available alabaster export functionality. qdata: content object's @qdata slot (present). Currently SummarizedExperiment objects supported. sample_data: object's sampleData data frame. sample_data_links: content object's @sampleDataLinks slot defining mapping rows sampleData entities object, e.g. spectra. sample_data_links_mcols: metadata content @sampleDataLinks. spectra: Spectra::Spectra object MS data (present). respective SpectraStash::SpectraStash functionality used export data. Note MsBackend types might supported. case, backend switched one MsBackends Spectra package using Spectra::setBackend() function.

title: MsExperiment Stash — MsExperimentStash
text: MsExperiment objects can also saved plain text files MsExperimentStash text file format. Note however currently object's content can saved format. Content slots @qdata, @metadata @otherData (present) stored stash. text file-based stash directory contains following files: ms_experiment_sample_data.txt: tabulator delimited text file content MsExperiment's sampleData(). file always saved. ms_experiment_sample_data_links_.txt: two column tab-delimited text file mapping rows sampleData() elements slots MsExperiment. name respective slot used file name suffix. file generated sample data links present. ms_experiment_link_mcols.txt: tab-delimited text file metadata content @sampleDataLink slot. file generated sample data links present. ms_experiment_files.txt: object's MsExperiment::MsExperimentFiles. See MsExperimentFilesStash information format. file generated experimentFiles() present object. MsExperiment contained Spectra::Spectra object, saved main MsExperimentStash folder. different set files might stored, depending MsBackend used. can also include raw MS data files parameter consolidate = TRUE used saveMsObject(). See SpectraStash::SpectraStash information.

title: MsExperiment Stash — MsExperimentStash
text: addition MsExperimentStash formats storage MsExperiment objects, possible load data metabolomics study directly MetaboLights repository. See MetaboLightsParam information.

title: MsExperiment Stash — MsExperimentStash
text: Philippine Louail, Johannes Rainer Philippine Louail

title: MsExperiment Stash — MsExperimentStash
code: ## Example MS data files library(Spectra) #> Loading required package: S4Vectors #> Loading required package: stats4 #> Loading required package: BiocGenerics #> Loading required package: generics #>  #> Attaching package: ‘generics’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     as.difftime, as.factor, as.ordered, intersect, is.element, setdiff, #>     setequal, union #>  #> Attaching package: ‘BiocGenerics’ #> The following object is masked from ‘package:fs’: #>  #>     path #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     IQR, mad, sd, var, xtabs #> The following object is masked from ‘package:utils’: #>  #>     data #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     Filter, Find, Map, Position, Reduce, anyDuplicated, aperm, append, #>     as.data.frame, basename, cbind, colnames, dirname, do.call, #>     duplicated, eval, evalq, get, grep, grepl, is.unsorted, lapply, #>     mapply, match, mget, order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int, #>     rank, rbind, rownames, sapply, saveRDS, scale, sequence, table, #>     tapply, transform, unique, unsplit, which.max, which.min #>  #> Attaching package: ‘S4Vectors’ #> The following object is masked from ‘package:utils’: #>  #>     findMatches #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     I, expand.grid, unname #> Loading required package: BiocParallel library(MsExperiment) library(MsDataHub) fls <- c(X20171016_POOL_POS_1_105.134.mzML(),     X20171016_POOL_POS_3_105.134.mzML()) #> see ?MsDataHub and browseVignettes('MsDataHub') for documentation #> loading from cache #> see ?MsDataHub and browseVignettes('MsDataHub') for documentation #> loading from cache  ## Create a MsExperiment for the two example files mse <- readMsExperiment(fls, data.frame(name = c("A", "B"), index = 1:2))  ## Define the path where to create the MsExperimentStash d <- file.path(tempdir(), "ms_experiment_stash")  ## Save the MsExperiment to a stash in alabaster format; Note: with ## `consolidate = TRUE` the MS data files are also copied into the ## stash saveMsObject(mse, AlabasterParam(d), consolidate = TRUE)  ## Show the content of the stash folder library(fs) dir_tree(d) #> /tmp/RtmpB00ZqG/ms_experiment_stash #> ├── OBJECT #> ├── _environment.json #> ├── experiment_files #> │   ├── OBJECT #> │   └── x #> │       ├── OBJECT #> │       └── list_contents.json.gz #> ├── metadata #> │   ├── OBJECT #> │   └── list_contents.json.gz #> ├── other_data #> │   ├── OBJECT #> │   └── list_contents.json.gz #> ├── sample_data #> │   ├── OBJECT #> │   └── basic_columns.h5 #> ├── sample_data_links #> │   ├── OBJECT #> │   ├── list_contents.json.gz #> │   └── other_contents #> │       └── 0 #> │           ├── OBJECT #> │           └── array.h5 #> ├── sample_data_links_mcols #> │   ├── OBJECT #> │   └── basic_columns.h5 #> └── spectra #>     ├── OBJECT #>     ├── backend #>     │   ├── 10883dc0fa5b_7860 #>     │   ├── 108862219686_7859 #>     │   ├── OBJECT #>     │   └── spectra_data #>     │       ├── OBJECT #>     │       └── basic_columns.h5 #>     ├── metadata #>     │   ├── OBJECT #>     │   └── list_contents.json.gz #>     ├── processing #>     │   ├── OBJECT #>     │   └── contents.h5 #>     ├── processing_chunk_size #>     │   ├── OBJECT #>     │   └── contents.h5 #>     ├── processing_queue_variables #>     │   ├── OBJECT #>     │   └── contents.h5 #>     └── spectra_processing_queue.json  ## Restore the object from the stash res <- readMsObject(MsExperiment(), AlabasterParam(d)) res #> Object of class MsExperiment  #>  Spectra: MS1 (1862)  #>  Experiment data: 2 sample(s) #>  Sample data links: #>   - spectra: 2 sample(s) to 1862 element(s).  sampleData(res) #> DataFrame with 2 rows and 3 columns #>                          name     index spectraOrigin #>                   <character> <integer>   <character> #> 108862219686_7859           A         1 /github/ho... #> 10883dc0fa5b_7860           B         2 /github/ho...  spectra(res) #> MSn data (Spectra) with 1862 spectra in a MsBackendMzR backend: #>        msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>      <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1            1     0.280         1 #> 2            1     0.559         2 #> 3            1     0.838         3 #> 4            1     1.117         4 #> 5            1     1.396         5 #> ...        ...       ...       ... #> 1858         1   258.636       927 #> 1859         1   258.915       928 #> 1860         1   259.194       929 #> 1861         1   259.473       930 #> 1862         1   259.752       931 #>  ... 25 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> 108862219686_7859 #> 10883dc0fa5b_7860
